publicSurface.rule: unsoccer/index.html is the public static page for the local server and production route until the built client is deployed there.
UI Designer owns public page hierarchy, prototype status copy, catalog continuity, and itch.io presentation fit.
The public route must not expose a directory listing.

rules.rule: Client bundles must not import server-only code.

v0.0.002 keeps Russian player-facing text, HDR-style environment lighting, a visible sun, a 120-second realtime day cycle, an inertial perspective follow camera, and distinct server-authoritative body/left-foot/right-foot contacts.
v0.0.003 keeps weather and hazards server-authoritative; any puddle, slush, or snowbank that changes movement or ball physics must also be visible in the client.
v0.0.007 aligns public client/server version and keeps production transport dependency-free: built-in Node WebSocket JSON plus HTTP polling fallback.
v0.0.008 keeps the version and client weight always visible and starts the residential courtyard visual pass around the snowy field.

v0.0.008 audio uses a server-authored audioEvents ring buffer in ServerState so roster, kick/body, goal, and countdown cues synchronize across WebSocket and HTTP fallback clients by authoritative event id.

v0.0.009 expands the runtime Art Director pass with courtyard models, procedural animated footballer rigs, visible sun/moon/orbit marker, ambient/bounce lighting, and QA-readable art/day-cycle datasets.
v0.0.009 makes WASD team-relative, keeps local controlled movement responsive with a short client prediction lead, and removes artificial pitch bounds from player movement.

v0.0.009 ships the UI Designer runtime pass with redesigned HUD hierarchy, event/status/network panels, toolbar buttons, settings modal tabs, localStorage settings, remappable input, runtime audio/graphics/network/accessibility controls, and QA-readable UI debug datasets.
v0.0.010 is the unified 0010 release build with all current runtime workstreams, a camera-framed sun/moon marker, synchronized version surfaces, and repo-side publication evidence.

v0.0.011 doubles the field/courtyard, starts authoritative time at 06:00, rotates sun/moon by server day time, randomizes weather every 60-120s, adds dawn birds/day traffic audio, moving cars, sprint/stamina/exhaustion/jump, foot/hand/head player hits, bouncier ball physics, thicker goal rebounds, local cloth net ripples, and 10 local Free3D vertex-color soccer-ball GLBs.

v0.0.012 keeps the current scene size, fixes server-driven day cycle under qaTime=0, makes 04:00-20:00 bright daytime and 20:00-04:00 the dark window, hides the old sun-path debug line, prevents clear weather precipitation particles, weights random weather toward clear/dawn, upgrades the goal net to client-only Verlet cloth, shows the active Free3D vertex-color GLB ball on-field while hiding that rack variant, aligns visible goal posts with thicker server colliders, and acceptance-tests post rebounds plus bouncier heading physics.

v0.0.013 replaces the primary primitive player body with a local Free3D Online 6300420 skinned GLB character optimized to 2.09 MB, cloned with SkeletonUtils, animated with per-player AnimationMixer, and keeps procedural primitives only as loading/error fallback.

v0.0.014 replaces the white all-animation character runtime with a different textured Free3D 6299851 split pipeline: rigged_unity.glb, WebP albedo/normal/ORM maps, and separate FBX idle/walk/run/jump clips selected by player state.

v0.0.015 extracts the skinned character runtime into client/src/character-controller.ts, adds character-controller-test.html, blends idle/walk/run/jump by velocity with hysteresis/time scaling, applies procedural bone-IK strike overlays, extends the authoritative day cycle to 300 seconds, shortens true dark hours to 23:00-03:00, and adds four night-only floodlight masts with local volumetric beam meshes.

v0.0.016 halves goal post and crossbar radius, switches posts to neutral light metal, scores goals only on front goal-line plane crossing from the field side, rejects back-net entries, and adds clear center-circle, center-spot, penalty-box, goal-area, and penalty-spot markings.
v0.0.017 adds the local stamina HUD meter with state colors/labels, exposes local stamina datasets, and makes hand hits readable with a longer orange strike trail plus hand-action datasets.

v0.0.017 character input/strike correction uses middle mouse button for head hits, prevents mouse wheel from triggering attacks, sends foot/hand ball impulses forward along player yaw from the limb-specific contact point, and alternates hand-hit visual/contact side without adding a new network KickKind.
v0.0.018 keeps the camera anchored to the controlled player with smoothed velocity lead, removes ball-driven camera drift, and adds HUD offscreen arrows for the ball and other players.

v0.0.018 character roster extension expands the local runtime roster to 11 texture+animation characters and makes character-controller-test cycle them with arrow keys plus UI; Free3D entries require local rigged_unity.glb, WebP albedo/normal/ORM maps, and FBX idle/walk/run/jump clips, while AutoRig task entries may use local textured animations.glb plus local FBX preview clips when full downloads are locked.
v0.0.019 extends local-only goal nets into closed cages with back, roof, left-side, and right-side Verlet panels per goal while keeping net motion off the network.
v0.0.020 halves the authoritative shared BALL_RADIUS from 0.48 to 0.24 so the server collider, visible client ball, Free3D ball scale, sideline rack, and acceptance fixtures stay in sync.
v0.0.021 delays goal reset: 5 seconds of scoring-team celebration, then a 1-second server-authored ball return to kickoff before countdown; never teleport the ball to center immediately after a goal.
v0.0.021 balances the half-size ball with BALL_DENSITY 3.6 and lower normal hit impulses; acceptance guards against runaway projectile speeds.

v0.0.022 retargets Free3D character hand-strike IK so alternating hand hits keep logical left/right side, compensate mirrored roster arm bones, and punch forward at upper-chest/shoulder height with matching raised hand flash/trail.

v0.0.022 smooths authoritative keyboard movement: raw axes ramp, released axes decay, opposite axes take over faster, and controlled velocity accelerates/brakes; client prediction mirrors this and exposes data-movement-smoothing, data-local-move-speed, and data-local-move-axis for QA.
v0.0.023 assigns newly joined players random ready characters from CHARACTER_ROSTER through a shuffled non-repeating server deck and preserves player.characterId during role/team rebalancing.
v0.0.024 uses team-colored ground indicator rings and halos under players; browser QA exposes data-local-team-marker and data-local-team-marker-color for the local player.

v0.0.024 adds jumpRun for sprint/high-velocity jumps; until dedicated run-jump FBX clips are added to the runtime roster, jumpRun clones the normal jump clip and applies a stronger forward-leap IK overlay exposed through data-player-rig-jump-style=run.

v0.0.025 follows a lerped authoritative player offset for camera position/look target instead of skinned mesh or bone transforms; browser QA exposes data-camera-anchor-smoothing, data-camera-anchor-offset, and data-camera-follow-speed.

v0.0.025 activates server-authored player.ragdoll/ragdollAt when stamina reaches zero: sprint exhaustion preserves movement inertia, stamina-emptying hits apply heavy knockback/lift, local prediction must not override ragdoll snapshots, and browser QA exposes data-player-rig-ragdoll plus data-local-player-ragdoll.

v0.0.026 widens night floodlight coverage with broader SpotLight beams, larger volumetric cones, per-mast white-temperature variation, and subtle deterministic flicker exposed through data-stadium-light-beam-angle, data-stadium-light-beam-radius, data-stadium-light-palette, and data-stadium-light-flicker.

v0.0.027 makes player-ball contact height-aware: body bumps require vertical body overlap, foot/hand/head hits require vertical reach, jumps can clear ground balls, and unreachable high balls cannot be headed.

v0.0.028 extends the residential courtyard with outer roads, sidewalks, dense procedural props, and 8 local Free3D environment GLB asset sources cloned into 100+ environment instances; browser QA exposes data-environment-model-instances, data-procedural-environment-instances, data-free3d-environment-instances, data-free3d-environment-asset-count, and data-free3d-environment-loaded.

v0.0.029 doubles ordinary foot/hand/head ball-hit power and adds server-authored LMB left-foot charge from 2x tap power to 4x full power over one second; held charge may fire once on ball contact before release and browser QA exposes data-local-kick-charge.
v0.0.029 widens gameplay camera framing and adds close sideline pennant/bench strips so the dense v0.0.028 courtyard dressing is visible in normal play while the pitch remains clear.
Server-confirmed left-foot, hand, head, jump, and body contacts must stay visually distinguishable and expose data-last-action-kind, data-last-action-player, and data-last-action-at for QA.
Server owns physics, score, teams, and room assignment.

Production transport is plain WebSocket JSON under /unsoccer/socket/ with HTTP polling fallback, implemented with built-in Node http/net/crypto primitives; do not require geckos.io, ws, or native node-datachannel on the Qwertystock host.
UNSOCCER_TEST_MODE enables /api/test/* only for isolated local acceptance and must not be exposed in production.

npm run test:unsoccer:acceptance is the deterministic gate for spectator roles, team-relative WASD movement, smoothed keyboard axes/acceleration, HTTP fallback join/input/state, pitch-bound freedom, foot/hand/head kicks, jump, sprint/stamina, weather, goal reset, and server audioEvents.
Acceptance derives the expected version from package.json.games.unsoccer.version and must not hard-code a release string.
npm run package:unsoccer rebuilds before creating dist/unsoccer-itch.zip so stale dist output is not published.
Client audio is procedural Web Audio driven by authoritative ServerState audioEvents and weather state, not local input prediction.
Audio QA must inspect data-audio-played-events, data-audio-blocked-events, data-audio-context, and data-audio-unlocked after a real trusted browser gesture.
First 4 clients are players; clients 5-32 are spectators/testers.
Itch package contains static built client only.
Free3D runtime assets require local GLB files and provenance; tokenless LowPoly 1k assets must be selected only from exact public worker inventory paths.json entries.
Character ids in shared/src/index.ts CHARACTER_ROSTER must exist in client/public/assets/characters/free3d/roster.json with local textures and clips before they can be assigned by the server.
AutoRig task character provenance must record the task page, task API metadata, animations.glb source, locked bundle/download status, and shipped local FBX preview clips.
